When the arrow key is pressed, the Spotify window changes position as expected, but is a still image. The music continues, but the timer freezes. If the song changes, the Spotify window does not update to reflect the new song's cover art. Clicking on the UI either does nothing or the click passes through the Spotify window to the window (or desktop) beneath. The pass-through behavior is difficult to reproduce. This bug is 100% reproducable but the mouse behavior can vary. In either case the UI stops updating and clicks do not work (change track, like/dislike, clicking a playlist to change the view, etc) Steps to reproduce: 1) Open Spotify in Windows on a multi-monitor computer 2) Ensure Spotify is maximized/full-screen 3) Play a song song (not necessary to trigger the bug, but you won't see a frozen timer otherwise) 4) Press WIN+SHIFT+RIGHT ARROW 5) Observe the Spotify window Bug on a multi-monitor Windows 10 setup: Moving a full-screen Spotify window between monitors using WIN+SHIFT+[Arrow key] causes the window to freeze and become unresponsive to user input until the window is repositioned using a different method.

NLS Bard was the one with a specific VO glitch (it doesn’t recognize the imported Braille files for some reason). It works fine when you download BRF from BARD itself. Read2Go has an internal problem. The reader works by selecting sections of text, and bookmarking it. Using a braille device won’t tell the app to select text, so therefore you can’t bookmark. So readers get bumped around the book. I have a 6+ and I tested the apps on both iOS 10 and 11 apps are up to date. NLS BARD Pro: can read BARD Braille files, book mark, navigate, etc. Con: small selection of braille, glitch when using voice over when reading braille files not downloaded from BARD (no ) Read2Go. Pro: direct download from Con: can’t bookmark, auto pushes me to the beginning of book sections periodically. Can’t find place again. Follow up. I’ve spent the day talking to coworkers. Here’s what I’m trying to do: replace my notetaker with my iPhone so I can carry less junk. To do this, I need a way to read my books in Braille on the phone. Oh, and I tested everything on iOS 11 and 10 just in case it was an update error. I can give up on using bookshare and just get all of my books through kindle, which will have the occasional image glitches. Hopefully BARD fixes that weirdness with VoiceOver. Meaning I would still need to be able to know in advance what I’ll want access to throughout the day, since my Braille Translation software is based on my computer. And there will be a lot of transferring back and forth using OneDrive. So, can I replace my Braille note taker with my phone? Eh. Sort of. There will be no access to bookshare in any functional sense, and I can’t read braille files. But I can convert Braille files to print, then pdf. Then use kindle. Pro: reads seamlessly through pages, is a kindle and can access book marks etc. Con: no access to BARD and bookshare and braille files in general. Pro: less glitchy, saves place, can read all iBooks Con: no access to bookshare, changing pages is kind of a pain Dolphin Easy Reader I gave up because I needed to make an account with them to link to my bookshare and I don’t want more accounts. Voice Dream (not tried directly yet, researched) Pro: can mark place, download bookshare Con: navigation seems fussy, and it re-displays the last line on previous page
